checks.a_analysis: The output follows the source document very closely. Each paragraph and heading in the provided JSON sections corresponds to specific text found in the source PDF (Agenda, Minutes, or Presentation slides). No fabricated text or invented sections were identified.

The URLs captured in the 'Top 10 Clicks' table (corresponding to page 13 of the source) were cross-referenced with the source document. All URLs are present in the source and have been transcribed accurately. No phone numbers or email addresses were found in the source document to verify.

The output document is missing several entire pages from the presentation portion of the source PDF. Specifically, Page 5 (Title slide), Page 6 (Campaign Overview), Page 7 (Campaign Metrics), Page 9 (Campaign Airings), Page 14 (URLs Accessed), and Page 15 (Reach and Analytics) are absent. The JSON explicitly contains a placeholder indicating that 6 sections were omitted due to token budget, which confirms the absence of substantial content from the source.
checks.b_evidence_source: Presentation pages 5 (Title), 6 (Campaign Overview), 7 (Spectrum Reach Campaign metrics), 9 (Campaign Airings), 14 (URLs Accessed), and 15 (Reach and Analytics).

checks.a_analysis: All tables present in the output were compared to their counterparts in the source PDF. This includes the Agenda table (p. 1), Attendees tables (p. 2), Roll Call Vote tables (p. 4), Demographics and City/Zip tables (p. 8), Analytics tables (p. 10-13), and the PSAP Impacts table (p. 16). In every case, the row counts match the source document exactly.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: Excluding the entirely missing pages (addressed in C3), the text within the provided sections is complete. Paragraphs from the Minutes (items 5, 6, 7, 8, 9) and the bulleted lists from the presentation slides (ROI) accurately reflect the source content.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: A text discrepancy was found in the 'Networks' list within the Durham Zone section (corresponding to page 8). The source document lists 'NICK' between 'NATG' and 'OUTD'. The output omits 'NICK' and instead repeats 'NATG', resulting in 'NATG, NATG, OUTD'. This is an error in transcription that alters the list of networks reported.
checks.b_evidence_source: Networks list on page 8: "...NFL, NATG, NICK, OUTD..."
checks.b_evidence_output: Networks paragraph in Section 5 (reading_order 51): "...NFL, NATG, NATG, OUTD..."

checks.a_analysis: The document contains repeated headers ('North Carolina 911 Board') and logos, but these reflect the multi-document nature of the source (Agenda, Minutes, Slides). No internal content blocks are erroneously duplicated within the conversion of single pages.
The hierarchy follows a logical flow from H1 (Title) to H2 (Major Section) and H3 (Subsection/Metadata). Slide titles are consistently mapped to H1. There are no skipped heading levels.

The table 'Zone Cities and Zips' on page 8 (reading_order 49) has significant row alignment and data corruption issues. Row 6 erroneously repeats '27508 | Bunn' in both halves of the row, whereas the source has '27508 | Bunn | 27553 | Manson'. Subsequent rows (e.g., Row 7) are misaligned as a result, pairing data from different horizontal lines in the source.
checks.b_evidence_source: Table on Page 8: Row 6 (27508 Bunn | 27553 Manson); Row 7 (27509 Butner | 27803 Rocky Mount).
checks.b_evidence_output: reading_order 49: Row 6 (27508 | Bunn | 27508 | Bunn); Row 7 (27509 | Butner | 27553 | Manson).

checks.a_analysis: The reading order of the output correctly follows the sequence of pages and the logical top-to-bottom flow within pages and slides.

Lists are generally identified correctly. Although numbered lists in the Agenda and ROI sections are converted to unordered lists in the JSON, the sequential meaning and item separation are preserved, which is acceptable under minor formatting differences.
URLs present in the source (specifically on slide 13) are correctly captured as text in the JSON. There are no broken, fabricated, or missing URL strings.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output maintains the logical organization of the source PDF. The agenda and minutes are presented as distinct sections, and the presentation slides are linearized into a single-column flow that preserves the sequence of the original deck. Table structures are preserved where appropriate, and multi-column slide content is logically grouped.

Tables from both the agenda/minutes and the presentation slides (e.g., Attendance, Roll Call, Top Referral Sites, PSAP Impacts) are rendered correctly as accessible HTML tables. They are readable, aligned, and have clear headers.

Multiple meaningful images and data visualizations from the source PDF are missing in the HTML output. Specifically, the map on page 8 (Durham Zone), the collage of network logos on page 9 (Campaign Airings), the line graph and pie chart on page 10 (Webpage Analytics), the bar chart on page 11 (Engagement), and the pie chart on page 12 (New Visitor vs Returning) are all absent. These are meaningful content items that convey data and context not fully captured by the remaining text.
checks.b_evidence_source: Meaningful images/charts on pages 8 (Map), 9 (Logo collage), 10 (Line graph, Pie chart), 11 (Bar chart), and 12 (Pie chart).
checks.b_evidence_output: The corresponding HTML sections for these slides (e.g., 'Durham (RLDU) Zone - 0664', 'Campaign Airings', 'Webpage Analytics: Site Summary') contain only text and tables, with the graphical visualizations completely omitted.
checks.b_evidence_location: Throughout the presentation slides section (starting at 'DRAFT - Recruitment Public Safety Announcement Campaign')

checks.a_analysis: There is no evidence of visual content duplication in the body of the document. Section headings appear in the navigation area (TOC) and the body, which is expected for accessible design.
